questions.question: What is meant by area in geometry?
questions.correct_answer: Area is the measure of the surface covered by a two-dimensional figure.
questions.explanation: It tells how much space lies inside the boundary of a shape.

questions.question: In which units is area measured?
questions.correct_answer: Square units such as cm², m², etc.
questions.explanation: Area measures surface coverage, so square units are used.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The number of unit squares inside a rectangle equals rows × columns.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a square?
questions.explanation: A square is a rectangle with equal sides.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a triangle?
questions.explanation: A triangle is half of a parallelogram with the same base and height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: A parallelogram can be rearranged to form a rectangle with the same base and height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rhombus using diagonals?
questions.explanation: The diagonals divide the rhombus into triangles.

questions.question: What is a trapezium?
questions.correct_answer: A quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides.
questions.explanation: The parallel sides are called bases.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a trapezium?
questions.correct_answer: Area = ½ × height × (sum of parallel sides)
questions.explanation: Derived by combining two trapeziums to form a parallelogram.

questions.question: How can the area of a polygon be calculated?
questions.correct_answer: By dividing it into triangles or rectangles and adding their areas.
questions.explanation: Complex shapes can be broken into simpler shapes.

notes.title: Concept of Area
notes.content: Area measures the surface covered by a shape and is expressed in square units.

notes.title: Triangle Area
notes.content: The area of a triangle is half the area of a parallelogram with the same base and height.

notes.title: Parallelogram Transformation
notes.content: A parallelogram can be cut and rearranged to form a rectangle with equal area.

notes.title: Rhombus Area Rule
notes.content: The area of a rhombus can be calculated using its diagonals.

notes.title: Area of Irregular Shapes
notes.content: Irregular shapes can be divided into simpler figures to calculate area.

facts.fact: Area is always measured in square units.
Two triangles with the same base and height have equal areas.
A parallelogram and a rectangle with the same base and height have equal areas.
The diagonals of a rhombus divide it into four triangles of equal area.
